text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared main repository. Each time code is added, a CI pipeline triggers automated builds and runs a series of tests. This process helps catch issues early, as problems are identified immediately rather than after days of work. By testing every change automatically, teams can find bugs and conflicts quickly. As a result, developers spend less time fixing errors and more time creating new features. CI improves collaboration, ensures code quality, and makes development faster and more reliable.
